volumeTexture Element

The <volumeTexture> element describes a 3D texture.

<volumeTexture
   id = string
   depth = integer
   filter = string
   format = string
   height = integer
   width = integer
   levels = integer
/>

Required Attributes

id

Texture identifier used for referencing from a <textureState> element. For more details, see XDX id Attribute.

depth

Depth in texels of the output volume texture resource. This attribute must be a power of two between 1 and 512. If depth is 1, the volume texture will a 2D texture that looks like a 3D texture to Direct3D.

Optional Attributes

filter

Specifies the filter used to resize the image.

If this value is specified, it must contain one of the following flags:

Value Description
D3DX_FILTER_NONE No scaling or filtering will take place. Pixels outside the bounds of the source image are assumed to be transparent black.
D3DX_FILTER_POINT Each destination pixel is computed by sampling the nearest pixel from the source image.
D3DX_FILTER_TRIANGLE Every pixel in the source image contributes equally to the destination image. This is the slowest of the filters. This is the default filter if nothing is specified.
D3DX_FILTER_LINEAR Each destination pixel is computed by sampling the four nearest pixels from the source image. This filter works best when the scale on both axes is less than two.
D3DX_FILTER_BOX Each pixel is computed by averaging a 2×2(×2) box of pixels from the source image. This filter works only when the dimensions of the destination are half those of the source, as is the case with mipmaps.

In addition, zero or more of the following flags may be ORed in with one of the flags above.

Value Description
D3DX_FILTER_WRAP Pixels will be wrapped, not mirrored. This is the default behavior.
D3DX_FILTER_MIRROR Specifying this flag is the same as specifying the D3DX_FILTER_MIRROR_U, D3DX_FILTER_MIRROR_V, and D3DX_FILTER_MIRROR_W flags.
D3DX_FILTER_MIRROR_U Pixels off the edge of the texture on the u-axis should be mirrored, not wrapped.
D3DX_FILTER_MIRROR_V Pixels off the edge of the texture on the v-axis should be mirrored, not wrapped.
D3DX_FILTER_MIRROR_W Pixels off the edge of the texture on the w-axis should be mirrored, not wrapped.
D3DX_FILTER_DITHER This flag indicates that the resulting image be dithered using a 4×4 ordered dither algorithm.
format

Format for the output texture. Must be a member of the D3DFORMAT enumeration; however, it cannot be a linear format or a depth/stencil format. If the output format is not specified, it will default to D3DFMT_A8R8G8B8.

height

Height in texels of the output texture. Must be a power of two between 1 and 512. If this attribute is not specified, height will be the largest height of the included slices. If the height specified or calculated is not a power of two, or does not equal the source image height, the image height will be scaled to the next largest power of two.

width

Width in texels of the output texture. Must be a power of two between 1 and 512. If this attribute is not specified, width will be the largest width of the included slices. If the width specified or calculated is not a power of two, or does not equal the source image width, the image width will be scaled to the next largest power of two.

levels

Number of mipmap levels to automatically generate. Must be between 0 and 10. To generate mipmaps all the way down to 1×1, use 0. For RGB textures, mipmaps are generated using a triangular filter with dithering. For UVL textures, mipmaps are generated using a point filter with dithering. The filtering used to generate the mipmaps cannot be changed using the XDX file.

Element Information

Parent Elements XDX
Child Elements volumeSlice

Examples

Simple usage

The following example demonstrates how to use the <volumeTexture> element:

<volumeTexture id="Leaf" width="256" height="256" depth="4" format="D3DFMT_DXT4" >
  <volumeSlice slice="0" source="textures/leaf0.bmp" />
  <volumeSlice slice="1" source="textures/leaf1.bmp" />
  <volumeSlice slice="2" source="textures/leaf2.bmp" /> 
  <volumeSlice slice="3" source="textures/leaf3.bmp" />
</volumeTexture >
